profile.background: Businessman and developer; Georgia state senator (2003-2007); Secretary of State (2010-2018); became 83rd governor in 2019.

profile.education: Georgetown University (BA Government), London School of Economics (MSc)
profile.background: Youngest sitting U.S. Senator. Documentary filmmaker and CEO of Insight TWI investigative media company. Former national security aide to Rep. Hank Johnson. Won 2021 runoff election.
profile.office: 317 Hart Senate Office Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Georgia delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-opensecrets.title: OpenSecrets — donor search for Jon Ossoff
aipac-opensecrets.excerpt: Non-partisan campaign-finance database. Search the candidate's name to see every FEC-reported donor, including pro-Israel lobby contributions.

aipac-endorsements.title: AIPAC Election Center — endorsed candidates
aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

profile.education: Morehouse College (BA Psychology), Union Theological Seminary (MDiv, MPhil, PhD)
profile.background: Senior pastor of Ebenezer Baptist Church, Martin Luther King Jr.'s congregation. First Black senator from Georgia. Eleventh of twelve children. Community organizer and civil rights advocate.
profile.office: 717 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Fani Willis
candidates.notes: In office since Jan 2021. Re-elected Nov 2024, term through 2029. Removed from Trump election interference case by GA Court of Appeals (Dec 2024) for appearance of impropriety, upheld by GA Supreme Court (Sep 2025). Peter Skandalakis took over Trump case. Willis remains DA for all other matters.
